message:
🐛 fix(missions): persist sidebar open/closed state instead of guessing from mission status (#8436)

The sidebar kept re-opening on every refresh because the init logic
inferred open/closed from the active mission's status — a heuristic
that breaks in demo mode (stale localStorage has 'running' status
from a previous interaction, even though loadMissions() will replace
it with fresh demo data milliseconds later).

User's rule: "if it was open before refresh, open it. If closed,
keep it closed." No heuristics, no mission-status guessing.

Fix: persist the sidebar's actual open/closed boolean to
localStorage (key: kc_mission_sidebar_open) on every state change.
On load, read it back. Simple, predictable, works identically in
demo mode and live mode.

Replaces the terminal-status cross-check from #8428 with a direct
boolean persistence. The ACTIVE_MISSION_STORAGE_KEY is still used
for restoring which mission is selected (transcript view), just no
longer for inferring sidebar visibility.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
